title: InsForge: Backend-as-a-Service for AI Agents
description: A guided tour of InsForge, an open-source BaaS platform that provides AI coding agents with authentication, database, storage, edge functions, and more.

steps.title: Welcome to InsForge
steps.description: InsForge is a monorepo Backend-as-a-Service platform built for AI coding agents and editors. It provides a semantic layer between AI agents and backend primitives — auth, PostgreSQL, S3 storage, serverless functions, and an LLM model gateway. The codebase is organized as npm workspaces: backend, frontend, auth, shared-schemas, and ui.

steps.title: Backend API Server
steps.description: The Express.js backend is the heart of InsForge. It starts from server.ts and is organized by feature — 16 route modules handle auth, database, storage, functions, AI, realtime, and more. Services follow a singleton pattern with dependency injection.

steps.title: Infrastructure & Providers
steps.description: The backend separates infrastructure concerns into two layers. The 'infra' directory handles database connections, socket management, realtime engines, and configuration. The 'providers' directory abstracts external services — storage backends, OAuth providers, and database drivers — making them swappable.

steps.title: Frontend Admin Dashboard
steps.description: The React 19 frontend is the admin dashboard for managing InsForge projects. Built with Vite, Tailwind CSS 4, and Radix UI, it uses a feature-driven architecture. Each feature module (AI, Database, Storage, Functions, etc.) mirrors a backend capability. TanStack Query handles server state, and Socket.io provides real-time updates.

steps.title: Shared Schemas & UI Library
steps.description: Type safety across the stack is enforced through shared-schemas — Zod schemas that serve as the single source of truth for all API models, request/response shapes, and domain types. The ui package provides a Radix UI-based component library with Tailwind presets shared by both the frontend dashboard and the auth app.

steps.title: Deployment & Configuration
steps.description: InsForge uses Docker multi-stage builds for production and docker-compose for development. The deploy directory contains platform-specific configs for Railway, Zeabur, and cloud providers. CI/CD runs through GitHub Actions. The project supports PostgreSQL, PostgREST for auto-REST APIs, and Deno for edge functions.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: InsForge brings together a TypeScript monorepo with five workspaces: the Express backend (port 7130), React dashboard (7131), auth UI (7132), shared Zod schemas, and a Radix UI library. It's a complete BaaS platform purpose-built for AI agents — giving them structured access to databases, storage, auth, serverless functions, and LLM gateways.
